title: tetragonia portulacoides
intro: Tetragonia portulacoides, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōka, is a succulent plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It is a type of green leafy vegetable that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, including iron, calcium, and potassium, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as anemia, digestive issues, and skin problems. Tetragonia portulacoides can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or poultice, and is also used in cooking as a nutritious and flavorful vegetable.
